name: dual_narrative_leak_cloud_onprem_terms
description:
Pins DUAL_NARRATIVE_MISLABEL via the SYMMETRIC cloud-half leak branch (engine/validator.py:673-680) — the second of the two phrase-table detectors in validate_dual_narratives. While dual_narrative_leak_advisory.json pins the on-prem-half provider-only branch (validator.py:665-672), this case pins the cloud-half on-prem-only branch: a cloud narrative containing 'physical data center' (the third entry in _ONPREM_ONLY_PHRASES at validator.py:171-180) fires the leak NOTE, appends to dual_result.notes, and adds RejectionReason.DUAL_NARRATIVE_MISLABEL to dual_result.flagged.

Why the symmetric branch matters: _ONPREM_ONLY_PHRASES is a deliberately narrower table than _PROVIDER_ONLY_PHRASES because the cloud half is legitimately allowed to reference local integrations ('syncs to on-prem Splunk', 'forwards logs to the local SIEM'). Only the strongest tells — 'physical data center', 'rack-mounted', 'on-prem servers' — fire. Without this case, deleting the cloud-side check (e.g. removing the `if cloud and _has_any(cloud_lower, _ONPREM_ONLY_PHRASES):` block at validator.py:673) would let an LLM swap-the-halves error in the cloud direction pass silently while the on-prem direction still gets caught — half the bidirectional protection vanishes.

Isolation -- nothing else can fire and confuse the assertion:
  - No CRM responsibility supplied -> assessor.py threads crm_resp_for_check=None -> CRM cross-check branch at validator.py:685-712 is entirely skipped. Only the phrase-table detectors run.
  - narrative_on_prem is clean of every _PROVIDER_ONLY_PHRASES token (no 'aws', 'azure', 'gcp', 'csp', 'cloud service provider', 'implemented by', 'inherited from', 'aws govcloud', 'azure government', 'fedramp-authorized csp'). Only ONE flag accumulates -> dual_narrative_flags_contains: ['dual_narrative_mislabel'] is satisfied by exactly one detector firing.
  - Column Q narrative ('Examined the program account management documentation...') is independently valid against the main validate() pass:
    - Affirming phrases 'Examined ' (validator.py:57), 'confirmed via' (line 44), 'documented in' (line 54). classes_hit=1 -> _classify_single returns COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING.
    - _expected_status_for_class(COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING) -> COMPLIANT; proposed Compliant matches -> no STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH.
    - Column Q does NOT contain 'inherited from' -> MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER is irrelevant (the regex only fires when 'inherited from' is present without a source token).
    - Token-set Jaccard vs procedures ('Examine documented account management plan; confirm enumerated account types approved by designated approval authority.') stays well below _OVERLAP_THRESHOLD=0.70 -- column Q adds USD reference, AO signing, section number; procedures is 12 tokens.
    - No SV-#####r#_rule -> UNCORROBORATED_STIG_PASS skipped.
    - No future-tense -> FUTURE_TENSE_COMPLIANCE skipped.
    - USD00099999 appears in tagged_evidence -> UNSUPPORTED_DOC_CITATION skipped.
    - Narrative does not contain _HYBRID_SPLIT_RE markers ('On-prem:'/'Cloud:'/'## responsibility_split') so _split_hybrid_narrative returns None -> classify_narrative takes the single-block path, no merge ladder involved. The dual halves live in narrative_on_prem/narrative_cloud (UI-fidelity fields), not embedded in column Q.
  - Procedures (col K) does not name a CSP -> Rule 8b doesn't pre-empt the LLM. Procedures does not contain 'auto-compliant per' or any Rule 8a marker.
  - tagged_evidence is non-empty so Step 1.65 no-evidence short-circuit (assessor.py:334) does not force NON_COMPLIANT.
  - Single LLM proposal accepted on first try -> source='llm', llm_calls=1, needs_review=False. The advisory dual-narrative flag does NOT propagate into the retry budget or the human-review queue (validator.py docstring: 'Advisory only -- column Q already passed validate() and is the canonical export slot').

Mutation differentiation -- neutralizing the cloud-half leak check at validator.py:673-680 (e.g. `if False and cloud and ...`):
  - dual_result.flagged would no longer contain DUAL_NARRATIVE_MISLABEL.
  - Runner's dual_narrative_flags_contains assertion at test_eval_harness.py would fail loudly: 'expected dual_narrative_flag dual_narrative_mislabel, got []' (or similar).
  - On-prem leak case (dual_narrative_leak_advisory) STILL passes because that branch lives at validator.py:665-672 -- so the on-prem detector alone cannot mask the cloud-side regression. Both directions must fire to maintain bidirectional protection.

Alternative mutation -- removing 'physical data center' from _ONPREM_ONLY_PHRASES:
  - cloud half no longer matches any phrase -> flagged stays empty.
  - Same assertion failure mode -> still caught.

Control AC-2.1 / CCI-000015 (Account Management) substrate matches the established dual_narrative_leak_advisory case: AC-2 is the canonical control for both the column-Q-split renderer and the leak detectors, so the two cases (provider-leak-on-onprem and onprem-leak-on-cloud) form a clean sibling pair under the same control family. The mislabel itself is realistic: an LLM that retrieves both the on-prem account enrollment workflow AND a continuity narrative about an offline break-glass roster could legitimately confuse which scope owns the 'physical data center' reference -- exactly the kind of swap-the-halves error this detector exists to catch.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization defines and documents the account types permitted within the information system.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine documented account management plan; confirm enumerated account types approved by designated approval authority.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00099999 Program Account Management Plan Rev D — enumerates account types and AO approval record.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined the program account management documentation USD00099999; confirmed via section 3 that account types are documented in the approval record signed by the AO.
llm_stub_proposals.narrative_on_prem: Customer manages local account enrollment and quarterly recertification at the program's primary workstation tier; account types are approved by the designated approval authority per USD00099999.
llm_stub_proposals.narrative_cloud: Cloud IAM control plane enforces the same account-type baselines; the customer's physical data center maintains an offline copy of the privileged-account roster for break-glass continuity.
